requirements.title: Stale index classification by filesystem existence
requirements.body: When `find_stale_indexes` scans the symbol store, each `index_*.json` file MUST be classified into exactly one of three stable reasons: `repo_path_enoent` (the `repo_path` field references a path that does not exist on disk), `unreadable_index_json` (the file exists but is not valid JSON), or `missing_repo_path_field` (the file is valid JSON but lacks a `repo_path` key). A file whose `repo_path` references an existing directory MUST NOT be classified as stale. Transient filesystem errors (e.g., `PermissionError` during `is_dir()`) MUST be treated as 'preserve, do not classify' rather than as stale.

scenarios.title: repo_path field references deleted path
given: a symbol index file `index_abc.json` exists in the storage directory
the file is valid JSON with `{"repo_path": "/tmp/deleted-repo", ...}`
the directory `/tmp/deleted-repo` does not exist on disk
then: the file is classified with reason `repo_path_enoent`
the file appears in the returned stale list

scenarios.title: Malformed JSON in index file
given: a symbol index file `index_xyz.json` exists
the file contents are not valid JSON (truncated or malformed)
then: the file is classified with reason `unreadable_index_json`
the file appears in the returned stale list

scenarios.title: Valid JSON without repo_path key
given: a symbol index file `index_def.json` exists
the file is valid JSON but lacks a `repo_path` key
then: the file is classified with reason `missing_repo_path_field`
the file appears in the returned stale list

scenarios.title: Healthy index not classified as stale
given: a symbol index file `index_ok.json` exists
the file is valid JSON with `repo_path: /existing/repo`
the directory `/existing/repo` exists on disk
then: the file is NOT classified as stale
the file does not appear in the returned stale list

requirements.title: Delete-time guards: path-confinement, TOCTOU, grace window, batch isolation
requirements.body: When `dry_run=False`, `prune_symbols` MUST enforce four guards: (1) path-confinement — the resolved path of each index file MUST be strictly under the resolved storage root; tampered paths outside the root MUST be refused and recorded in `failures[]`; (2) TOCTOU — symlinks MUST be refused at scan time (skip silently) and at delete time (record in `failures[]`); (3) grace window — entries whose mtime is within `LGREP_PRUNE_MIN_AGE_S` seconds (default 3600, overridable via env var) AND whose reason is `unreadable_index_json` MUST be preserved; reasons `repo_path_enoent` and `missing_repo_path_field` bypass the grace check (they are unambiguous); (4) batch isolation — per-entry `unlink` failures MUST be captured in `failures[]` with the file path and error message, and MUST NOT abort processing of remaining entries.

scenarios.title: Path-confinement refuses out-of-root paths at delete time
given: a stale index whose resolved path resolves to `/etc/passwd` (outside storage root)
then: the entry is refused
the entry appears in `failures[]` with an explanatory error
the file `/etc/passwd` is not deleted

scenarios.title: TOCTOU refuses symlinks at delete time
given: a stale index whose file is a symlink to another location
then: the entry is refused
the entry appears in `failures[]`
the symlink target is not deleted

scenarios.title: Grace window preserves recent unreadable_index_json
given: a stale index with reason `unreadable_index_json`
the file's mtime is 60 seconds ago
LGREP_PRUNE_MIN_AGE_S is set to 3600 (default)
then: the entry is NOT deleted
the entry does not appear in `deleted_dirs` (or equivalent count field)
the entry is noted as grace-preserved in the report

scenarios.title: Per-entry unlink failure does not abort batch
given: three stale index files in the storage directory
the second file raises `OSError` on `unlink()`
then: the first file is deleted
the second file appears in `failures[]` with the OSError message
the third file is deleted (processing continues)

requirements.title: lgrep gc umbrella invokes prune_symbols and nests result in combined report
requirements.body: The `lgrep gc` command MUST invoke `prune_symbols` alongside the existing `prune_orphans` and `gc_worktree_meta` sweeps. The combined report dict MUST nest the prune_symbols result under a new top-level key spelled exactly `prune_symbols` (snake_case, matching the existing `prune_orphans` and `gc_worktree_meta` key convention). Existing report keys (`prune_orphans`, `gc_worktree_meta`) MUST be preserved with their respective shapes and values unchanged.

scenarios.title: gc combined report nests prune_symbols alongside existing keys
given: the `lgrep gc` command is invoked with default flags
then: the combined report contains exactly the top-level keys `prune_orphans`, `gc_worktree_meta`, and `prune_symbols`
the `prune_orphans` and `gc_worktree_meta` values match what those functions return when invoked standalone
the `prune_symbols` value matches what `prune_symbols(...)` returns when invoked standalone with equivalent arguments

requirements.title: Dry-run default on every surface with reclaimed_bytes projection
requirements.body: The core `prune_symbols(...)` function and every operator-facing surface (CLI subcommand `lgrep prune-symbols`, MCP tool `prune_symbols`) MUST default to `dry_run=True` when no explicit argument is provided. The dry-run response MUST include a `reclaimed_bytes` field equal to the projected sum of stale entry file sizes (each `Path(entry).stat().st_size`). The CLI MUST treat `--execute` and `--dry-run` as mutually exclusive flags and exit non-zero with a stderr message when both are passed.

scenarios.title: Default invocation preserves disk and reports projected bytes
given: a storage directory with one stale index file of size 4096 bytes
scenarios.when: prune_symbols() is called with no arguments
then: no files are deleted from the storage directory
the returned report has `dry_run: true`
the returned report has `reclaimed_bytes: 4096`

scenarios.title: CLI rejects both --execute and --dry-run
given: the CLI subcommand `lgrep prune-symbols`
scenarios.when: invoked with both `--execute` and `--dry-run` flags simultaneously
then: the process exits with a non-zero status code
an error message is written to stderr explaining the mutual exclusion

requirements.title: Non-local (github:) entries skipped upfront
requirements.body: Entries whose `repo_path` field starts with the literal prefix `github:` MUST be skipped by `find_stale_indexes` before any stale classification logic runs. They MUST NOT appear in the returned stale list and MUST NOT be deleted by the execute path. These entries represent non-local indexes (e.g., `github:owner/name@ref`) with no local filesystem path to staleness-check.

scenarios.title: github: prefix skipped entirely
given: a symbol index file with `repo_path: github:owner/name@ref`
the file is otherwise valid
then: the file is NOT in the returned stale list
the file is preserved on disk after prune_symbols(dry_run=False) returns

requirements.title: MCP transport safety: non-stdio coerces dry_run=True
requirements.body: The MCP `prune_symbols` tool MUST coerce `dry_run=True` when the MCP transport is not stdio (i.e., HTTP, streamable-http, or unknown). Transport kind is sourced from the application lifespan context. Unknown transport MUST be treated as non-local (defensive default). The tool's user-facing description MUST direct callers to use the CLI (`lgrep prune-symbols --execute`) for destructive prunes on shared deployments, mirroring the `prune_orphans` MCP tool description convention.

scenarios.title: stdio transport allows caller-chosen dry_run
given: an MCP request invokes `prune_symbols` with `dry_run: false`
the transport is stdio
scenarios.when: the MCP handler processes the request
then: the request proceeds with `dry_run: false`
destructive deletion is allowed

scenarios.title: HTTP transport coerces dry_run=True
given: an MCP request invokes `prune_symbols` with `dry_run: false`
the transport is HTTP or streamable-http
scenarios.when: the MCP handler processes the request
then: the handler coerces `dry_run: true`
no files are deleted
the response indicates the coercion (e.g., `dry_run: true` in the report)
